_comment: Bad case 21 — Garbled-detection escalation. Real LSZB session: Whisper turned 'taxi' into 'Doxy' / 'TOCSY', rule parser hit UNKNOWN, the LM (in production) misclassified as INITIAL_CALL_TOWER / REQUEST_LANDING. Fix: rule parser UNKNOWN with no LM available falls into the 3-tier unclear handler. Tier 1 = recognisable elements present (callsign / runway / ATC keyword) -> 'transmission was garbled, say again'. Tier 2 = same again -> escalate with 'use standard phraseology'. Tier 3 = a clear utterance breaks the streak. Tests run without the LM (atc_repl path), exercising the LM-unavailable + UNKNOWN -> unclear-helper branch. The escalated 'use standard phraseology' line is what the controller hands the pilot in real life when they keep mangling phraseology.
name: Bad case 21 — Garbled / say again escalation (LSZB tower-only)

say.note: Tier 1 — Whisper killed 'taxi' to 'Doxy'. No facility keyword survived (matches real Whisper output where '-Tower' with dash failed has_facility_keyword). Rule parser returns UNKNOWN. Callsign + 'runway 14' + 'request' / 'information' keywords are recognisable -> garbled response.
say.text: Hotel Bravo Delta Charlie Hotel at the parking position request Doxy to runway 14 with information alpha

say.note: Tier 2 — same misfire again -> escalate to 'use standard phraseology'.
say.text: Hotel Bravo Delta Charlie Hotel request TOCSY to the runway 14

say.note: Reset — pilot finally gets 'taxi' right. Counter must reset so the next garbled call lands at tier 1 again.
say.text: Tower Hotel Bravo Delta Charlie Hotel at the parking position request taxi to runway 14 with information alpha
